author: Adware
text: Removal also INVALIDATES doctor work in sibling epic balls — clean when this lands: bl-8c0b (base-doctor-correction half is moot; keep/fold only the delivery-binding half), bl-cd21 (strike the tracker doctor-hook bullet). Stale incidental mentions to scrub so no agent rebuilds a hook: bl-20fc ('doctor already done, bl-d024' parenthetical), bl-f970 ('§16 doctor corrections' in its Blocks list), bl-30e2 ('EffectiveConfig + its doctor consumer'), bl-567a (doctor() = 1 of 3 worktree-path derive sites -> 2), bl-9773 (aligns prime to fn doctor() — alignment target gone, the cwd change itself still valid), bl-d074 (optional doctor.post backfill check for the status plugin — now impossible).

author: Adware
text: CLEANUP STATUS UPDATE: bl-8c0b now CLOSED (was worthless — doctor half dead, delivery half subsumed by bl-f970/bl-9773). Remaining doctor cleanup when this lands: (1) bl-cd21 — strike the tracker doctor-hook bullet (rest of ball live). (2) Scrub stale incidental mentions: bl-20fc, bl-f970, bl-30e2, bl-567a (3 worktree-path sites -> 2), bl-9773 (drop the 'align with fn doctor()' rationale, cwd change still valid), bl-d074 (optional doctor.post backfill no longer possible).

author: Unlearn
text: NOTE (bl-8540, merged e9b44cc): the registry->config conversion (bl-4e14) reworks/removes registry.rs's <op>/<phase> resolve + wired() — the same registry.rs you unwire doctor from here. Coordinate ordering with bl-4e14 to avoid a conflict on registry.rs. Doctor's check (a) 'missing bin/<name> for a wired plugin' stays valid: config/plugins/bin/<name> symlinks are unchanged; only the schedule moved to plugins.toml.

author: Awhile
text: SCOPE GAP — 4 files with doctor refs NOT in your target list, added/exposed after this ball was scoped (mostly by bl-20fc read verbs, landed main 81dae94/f2210b5): (1) tests/dispatch.rs L4,45,48,51 — uses 'doctor' as the example UNWIRED diffless verb, asserts stdout 'doctor: pre -> post'; (2) src/lib_tests.rs L27-28 — runs ['doctor'] asserting exit 0 (op-plan); (3) src/reads/tree.rs L9 — stale module-doc 'a blocker cycle is the doctor's concern, §16'; (4) src/delivery_tests.rs L179 — protocol ops list asserts [...,'prime','doctor']. cargo test WILL fail on (1)(2)(4) once Verb::Doctor is gone. NOTE: after doctor removal, 'install' is the ONLY remaining unwired diffless verb — retarget the (1)(2) examples to 'install'; and once install is wired too (bl-3dd9), the lib::run 'other => Op::plan' skeleton arm goes DEAD (uncovered) — that's the moment to delete the arm.

author: Awhile
text: UPDATE on my earlier scope-gap note: bl-c541 (closed, on main) already scrubbed 3 of the 4 — tests/dispatch.rs, src/lib_tests.rs, src/reads/tree.rs are now doctor-free (unwired-verb examples retargeted to 'install'; tree comment repointed to the readiness walk). REMAINING for you: only src/delivery_tests.rs L179 (protocol ops list asserts [...,'prime','doctor']) — left to you because it's coupled to delivery.rs's protocol declaration, which is already in your target list. Heads-up still stands: once install is wired (bl-3dd9) the lib::run 'other => Op::plan' arm goes dead.
